sections.paragraph: an unused root; Arabic عَدَّ to number, count, compute, especially days, time; Conj. IV. to determine, especially time. This verb appears to be secondary, and derived from the noun עַד time, like יָעַד, to which it is allied. Hence Syriac ܥܰܕܥܕܐܳ a festival day, i.q. מֹועֵד, Arabic عَدَّانُ, عِدَّدانُ, time, عِدَّةُ the time of the monthly courses of women, and Heb. עִדָּה, and also the pr.n. עִדֹּו, עַדְעָדָה Ch. עִדָּן.
